channel.title: Metadata on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Metadata on Pi Stack

item.title: OpenLineage vs DataHub vs Apache Atlas: Self-Hosted Data Lineage Guide 2026
item.description: Data lineage — the ability to track where data comes from, how it transforms, and where it ends up — has become essential for any organization running non-trivial data pipelines. When a dashboard shows a wrong number, lineage tells you exactly which upstream table, job, or transformation introduced the error. When regulators ask where personal data flows, lineage provides the audit trail.

item.title: Amundsen vs DataHub vs OpenMetadata: Self-Hosted Data Catalog Guide 2026
item.description: Modern organizations manage hundreds of databases, data lakes, warehouses, and streaming pipelines. Finding the right dataset, understanding its lineage, knowing who owns it, and trusting its quality are daily challenges for data teams. Commercial data catalogs like Alation, Collibra, and Informatica Enterprise Data Catalog charge premium prices and lock your metadata behind proprietary formats.
